title: Junellia tridens
intro: Junellia tridens, commonly known as 'Toloache', is a plant species belonging to the Verbenaceae family, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Junellia tridens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Junellia tridens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Junellia tridens is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It belongs to the genus Junellia, which is a group of shrubs and small trees native to South America. The family Verbenaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the verbena family. This family contains around 2,000 species, many of which are herbs, shrubs, or small trees. The classification of Junellia tridens reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Verbenaceae family.
